tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. IMPORTANT: Comments are subject to validation constraints enforced by the MCP server - maximum 65536 characters for the complete comment (including footer which is added automatically), 10 mentions (@username), and 50 links. Exceeding these limits will result in an immediate error with specific guidance. NOTE: By default, this tool requires discussions:write permission. If your GitHub App lacks Discussions permission, set 'discussions: false' in the workflow's safe-outputs.add-comment configuration to exclude this permission. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation. CONSTRAINTS: The complete comment (your body text + automatically added footer) must not exceed 65536 characters total. Maximum 10 mentions (@username), maximum 50 links (http/https URLs). A footer (~200-500 characters) is automatically appended with workflow attribution, so leave adequate space. If these limits are exceeded, the tool call will fail with a detailed error message indicating which constraint was violated.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool auto-targets the issue, PR, or discussion that triggered this workflow. Auto-targeting only works for issue, pull_request, discussion, and comment event triggers — it does NOT work for schedule, workflow_dispatch, push, or workflow_run triggers. For those trigger types, always provide item_number explicitly, or the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the issue or PR that triggered this workflow. Only works for issue or pull_request event triggers. For schedule, workflow_dispatch, or other triggers, item_number is required — omitting it will silently skip the label operation.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

{"number":57110,"title":"[MPG C# Generator] Paging action operations should not resource-wrap return types","body":"## Bug Description

The MPG C# generator incorrectly wraps paging results in resource types for **action** operations. When a paging operation is an ARM **action** (not a list), the generator should return the raw data type (e.g., `Pageable`), not the resource-wrapped type (e.g., `Pageable`).

## Reproduction

**Service:** CosmosDBForPostgreSql (`Microsoft.DBforPostgreSQL`)

**TypeSpec definition** (`ServerConfiguration.tsp`):

```typespec
@get
@action("configurations")
@list
listByServer is ArmResourceActionSync<
  ClusterServer,
  void,
  ArmResponse>
>;
```

This is an **action** on `ClusterServer`, not a list operation on `ServerConfiguration`/`NodeConfiguration`.

## tspCodeModel evidence

The emitter correctly identifies the operation as an **Action** in the resource metadata:

```json
{
    "methodId": "Microsoft.DBforPostgreSQL.Configurations.listByServer",
    "kind": "Action",
    "operationPath": ".../{clusterName}/servers/{serverName}/configurations",
    "resourceScope": ".../{clusterName}/servers/{serverName}"
}
```

However, the method-level metadata only says `kind: "paging"` — the Action vs List distinction is only in the resource operation metadata.

## Expected behavior

Since the operation is an **Action** (not a **List**), the generated method should return:

```csharp
public virtual Pageable GetConfigurations(...)
```

## Actual behavior

The generator sees the `paging` kind + a matching `NodeConfigurationResource` class, and produces:

```csharp
public virtual Pageable GetConfigurations(...)
```

## Root cause

The generator does not check the ARM resource operation `kind` (Action vs List) when deciding whether to resource-wrap paging results. It only checks the method-level `kind: "paging"` and the existence of a matching resource class.

## Current workaround

Use `CodeGenSuppress` in a `Customized/` file to suppress the generated method and replace it with a hand-written version returning the raw data type.

{"total_count":1,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4078984480,"number":57110,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"[MPG C# Generator] Paging action operations should not resource-wrap return types","body":"## Bug Description

The MPG C# generator incorrectly wraps paging results in resource types for **action** operations. When a paging operation is an ARM **action** (not a list), the generator should return the raw data type (e.g., `Pageable<ServerConfigurationData>`), not the resource-wrapped type (e.g., `Pageable<NodeConfigurationResource>`).

## Reproduction

**Service:** CosmosDBForPostgreSql (`Microsoft.DBforPostgreSQL`)

**TypeSpec definition** (`ServerConfiguration.tsp`):

```typespec
@get
@action("configurations")
@list
listByServer is ArmResourceActionSync<
  ClusterServer,
  void,
  ArmResponse<ResourceListResult<ServerConfiguration>>
>;
```

This is an **action** on `ClusterServer`, not a list operation on `ServerConfiguration`/`NodeConfiguration`.

## tspCodeModel evidence

The emitter correctly identifies the operation as an **Action** in the resource metadata:

```json
{
    "methodId": "Microsoft.DBforPostgreSQL.Configurations.listByServer",
    "kind": "Action",
    "operationPath": ".../{clusterName}/servers/{serverName}/configurations",
    "resourceScope": ".../{clusterName}/servers/{serverName}"
}
```

However, the method-level metadata only says `kind: "paging"` — the Action vs List distinction is only in the resource operation metadata.

## Expected behavior

Since the operation is an **Action** (not a **List**), the generated method should return:

```csharp
public virtual Pageable<CosmosDBForPostgreSqlServerConfigurationData> GetConfigurations(...)
```

## Actual behavior

The generator sees the `paging` kind + a matching `NodeConfigurationResource` class, and produces:

```csharp
public virtual Pageable<CosmosDBForPostgreSqlNodeConfigurationResource> GetConfigurations(...)
```

## Root cause

The generator does not check the ARM resource operation `kind` (Action vs List) when deciding whether to resource-wrap paging results. It only checks the method-level `kind: "paging"` and the existence of a matching resource class.

## Current workaround

Use `CodeGenSuppress` in a `Customized/` file to suppress the generated method and replace it with a hand-written version returning the raw data type.

{"total_count":61,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4055899539,"number":56935,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Add automated version bump pipeline for Azure Management Generator (AzureManagementGeneratorVersion)","body":"## Context

The Azure Generator (http-client-csharp) has an automated pipeline that bumps its version in `eng/centralpackagemanagement/Directory.Generation.Packages.props` whenever a new build is published. This happens through the `archetype-typespec-emitter.yml` template's Regenerate stage, which:

1. Publishes the new generator package to the Azure DevOps NuGet feed
2. Updates the emitter package JSON with the new version
3. Regenerates SDK clients
4. Creates a PR with title "Update {emitterName} version to prerelease {emitterVersion}"

The management generator (http-client-csharp-mgmt) should have the same automated version bump behavior for `AzureManagementGeneratorVersion`.

## Current State

- `UnbrandedGeneratorVersion` and `AzureGeneratorVersion` are automatically bumped by CI pipelines
- `AzureManagementGeneratorVersion` was recently added and is currently updated manually
- The mgmt generator CI (`eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/ci.yml`) already uses the same `archetype-typespec-emitter.yml` template, but the version bump for the NuGet package reference in `Directory.Generation.Packages.props` may not be wired up

## Expected Behavior

When a new version of the mgmt generator is published from the `http-client-csharp-mgmt` CI pipeline, `AzureManagementGeneratorVersion` in `Directory.Generation.Packages.props` should be automatically updated, mirroring how the Azure Generator pipeline updates `AzureGeneratorVersion`.

## Reference

- PR comment: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/56627#discussion_r2915155700
- Azure Generator CI: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/ci.yml`
- Mgmt Generator CI: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/ci.yml`
- Shared template: `eng/common/pipelines/templates/archetype-typespec-emitter.yml`
- Version props file: `eng/centralpackagemanagement/Directory.Generation.Packages.props`","author_association":"MEMBER","user":{"login":"ArcturusZhang","id":10554446,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEwNTU0NDQ2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10554446?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/ArcturusZhang","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/subscriptions"},"labels":[{"id":1178448111,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Mgmt","name":"Mgmt","color":"ffeb77","description":"This Generator Bug] Serialization uses undeclared variable additionalBinaryDataProperties0","body":"## Bug Description

In generated serialization code for flattened discriminated types, the deserialization method declares a variable named `additionalBinaryDataProperties` but the catch-all branch references `additionalBinaryDataProperties0` (with trailing `0`), causing CS0103 build errors.

## Impact

**89 files** affected in SecurityInsights migration (#56944). This blocks the entire migration.

## How to Reproduce

1. Clone `azure-sdk-for-net` and `azure-rest-api-specs`
2. Checkout branch `securityinsights-typespec-migration` in azure-sdk-for-net
3. Checkout branch `securityinsights-csharp-migration` in azure-rest-api-specs
4. Run code generation:
```bash
cd sdk/securityinsights/Azure.ResourceManager.SecurityInsights/src
dotnet build /t:GenerateCode /p:LocalSpecRepo=<path-to-azure-rest-api-specs>/specification/securityinsights/resource-manager/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/SecurityInsights
```
5. Build: `dotnet build`
6. Observe CS0103 errors in 89 `.Serialization.cs` files

## Root Cause

The bug occurs in types that have `@@Legacy.flattenProperty` in `back-compatible.tsp`. When the generator flattens a `properties` bag into the parent type, the serialization code generator appends a `0` suffix to the `additionalBinaryDataProperties` variable reference but not to its declaration.

**Example:** `Models/AATPDataConnector.Serialization.cs`

Declaration (line 117):
```csharp
IDictionary<string, BinaryData> additionalBinaryDataProperties = new ChangeTrackingDictionary<string, BinaryData>();
```

Usage (line 176) — **BUG**: `0` suffix added:
```csharp
additionalBinaryDataProperties0.Add(prop.Name, BinaryData.FromString(prop.Value.GetRawText()));
```

Constructor call (line 184) — no suffix:
```csharp
return new AATPDataConnector(id, name, resourceType, systemData, additionalBinaryDataProperties, kind, eTag, properties);
```

## Expected Behavior

The variable reference should match the declaration: `additionalBinaryDataProperties` (no `0` suffix).

## Affected Files (sample)

- `AATPDataConnector.Serialization.cs`
- `SecurityInsightsAccountEntity.Serialization.cs`
- `SecurityInsightsAadDataConnector.Serialization.cs`
- `MLBehaviorAnalyticsAlertRule.Serialization.cs`
- `SecurityInsightsFusionAlertRule.Serialization.cs`
- ... and 84 more

All affected types have `@@Legacy.flattenProperty(TypeName.properties)` in `back-compatible.tsp`.
","author_association":"MEMBER","user":{"login":"live1206","id":5196139,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUxOTYxMzk=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5196139?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/live1206","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/live1206","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/live1206/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/live1206/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/live1206/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/live1206/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/live1206/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/live1206/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/live1206/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/live1206/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/live1206/subscriptions"},"labels":[{"id":1178448111,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Mgmt","name":"Mgmt","color":"ffeb77","description":"This issue is related to a management package.","default":false,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMTc4NDQ4MTEx"},{"id":1508411177,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/CodeGen","name":"CodeGen","color":"e99695","description":"Issues that relate to code generation","default":false,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxNTA4NDExMTc3"}],"comments":1,"created_at":"2026-03-13T07:14:37Z","updated_at":"2026-03-13T19:13:02Z","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57054","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57054","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57054/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57054/events","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57054/labels{/name}","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net","parent_issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56944","reactions":{"total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":1,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57054/reactions"},"node_id":"I_kwDOACyxMM7yjfmZ"},{"id":4069394100,"number":57056,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"[Mgmt Generator Bug] Resource serialization instantiates abstract base type","body":"## Bug Description

The generator emits `new AbstractType()` in resource serialization helpers for polymorphic/discriminated base types. Since these types are `abstract`, this causes CS0144 build errors.

## Impact

**6 files** affected in SecurityInsights migration (#56944).

## How to Reproduce

1. Clone `azure-sdk-for-net` and `azure-rest-api-specs`
2. Checkout branch `securityinsights-typespec-migration` in azure-sdk-for-net
3. Checkout branch `securityinsights-csharp-migration` in azure-rest-api-specs
4. Run code generation:
```bash
cd sdk/securityinsights/Azure.ResourceManager.SecurityInsights/src
dotnet build /t:GenerateCode /p:LocalSpecRepo=<path-to-azure-rest-api-specs>/specification/securityinsights/resource-manager/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/SecurityInsights
```
5. Build: `dotnet build`
6. Observe CS0144 errors: `Cannot create an instance of the abstract type or interface`

## Root Cause

The generator creates a static deserialization instance field pattern for resource serialization. For polymorphic base types (discriminated unions), it tries to instantiate the abstract base class directly instead of using the static deserializer.

**Example:** `SecurityInsightsBillingStatisticResource.Serialization.cs` (line 19):
```csharp
private static IJsonModel<SecurityInsightsBillingStatisticData> DataDeserializationInstance 
    => s_dataDeserializationInstance ??= new SecurityInsights.SecurityInsightsBillingStatisticData();  // BUG: abstract type
```

But `SecurityInsightsBillingStatisticData` is declared as:
```csharp
public abstract partial class SecurityInsightsBillingStatisticData : ResourceData
```

## Expected Behavior

For abstract/polymorphic base types, the generator should use the base type's static `Deserialize` method or a non-abstract deserializer pattern instead of `new AbstractType()`.

## Affected Files

- `SecurityInsightsBillingStatisticResource.Serialization.cs` — `SecurityInsightsBillingStatisticData`
- `SecurityInsightsDataConnectorDefinitionResource.Serialization.cs` — `SecurityInsightsDataConnectorDefinitionData`
- `SecurityInsightsEntityQueryResource.Serialization.cs` — `SecurityInsightsEntityQueryData`
- `SecurityInsightsEntityQueryTemplateResource.Serialization.cs` — `SecurityInsightsEntityQueryTemplateData`
- `SecurityInsightsEntityResource.Serialization.cs` — `SecurityInsightsEntity`
- `SecurityInsightsSettingsResource.Serialization.cs` — `SecurityInsightsSettingsData`

Generator Bug] Generated code calls BinaryData.FromResponse which does not exist","body":"## Bug Description

The generator emits `BinaryData.FromResponse(result)` in resource operation methods, but `BinaryData` does not have a static `FromResponse` method. This causes CS0117 build errors.

## Impact

**4 files** affected in SecurityInsights migration (#56944).

## How to Reproduce

1. Clone `azure-sdk-for-net` and `azure-rest-api-specs`
2. Checkout branch `securityinsights-typespec-migration` in azure-sdk-for-net
3. Checkout branch `securityinsights-csharp-migration` in azure-rest-api-specs
4. Run code generation:
```bash
cd sdk/securityinsights/Azure.ResourceManager.SecurityInsights/src
dotnet build /t:GenerateCode /p:LocalSpecRepo=<path-to-azure-rest-api-specs>/specification/securityinsights/resource-manager/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/SecurityInsights
```
5. Build: `dotnet build`
6. Observe CS0117 errors: `BinaryData does not contain a definition for FromResponse`

## Root Cause

The generator treats `BinaryData` like a generated model type and emits a model-style `FromResponse` helper call. But `BinaryData` is a framework type that does not have this static method.

**Example:** `SecurityInsightsAutomationRuleResource.cs` (line 226):
```csharp
Response result = await Pipeline.ProcessMessageAsync(message, context).ConfigureAwait(false);
Response<BinaryData> response = Response.FromValue(BinaryData.FromResponse(result), result);  // BUG
```

## Expected Behavior

Should use `result.Content` instead:
```csharp
Response<BinaryData> response = Response.FromValue(result.Content, result);
```

## Affected Files

- `SecurityInsightsAutomationRuleResource.cs` (lines 226, 278)
- `SecurityInsightsIncidentResource.cs` (lines 617, 666)

These are resource types whose operations return `BinaryData` (e.g., delete operations with response bodies).

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This issue reports a bug in the **MPG (Management Plane Generator) C# Generator** where paging **action** operations are incorrectly resource-wrapping their return types. When a paging operation is an ARM **action** (not a list), the generator should return the raw data type (e.g., `Pageable<ServerConfigurationData>`), but instead produces a resource-wrapped type (e.g., `Pageable<NodeConfigurationResource>`).

Labels applied: `Mgmt`, `Central-EngSys`

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Analysis</summary>

### Root Cause
The generator checks `kind: "paging"` at the method level and the existence of a matching resource class, but does **not** check the ARM resource operation `kind` (Action vs List). The distinction between Action and List is only present in the resource operation metadata, not the method-level metadata.

### Reproduction
- **Service:** CosmosDBForPostgreSQL (`Microsoft.DBforPostgreSQL`)
- The TypeSpec defines a `@get @action("configurations") @list` operation using `ArmResourceActionSync`, which is an **action** on `ClusterServer`, not a list operation on `ServerConfiguration`.
- The emitter correctly marks this as `kind: "Action"` in the resource metadata, but the C# generator ignores this distinction.

### Fix Direction
The generator (in `/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt`) needs to:
1. Read the ARM resource operation `kind` field when deciding whether to resource-wrap paging return types.
2. Skip resource-wrapping when `kind == "Action"`, even if a matching resource class exists.

### Current Workaround
Use `CodeGenSuppress` in a `Customized/` file to suppress the generated method and replace it with a hand-written version returning the raw data type.

</details>

<details>
<summary>🔗 Relevant Resources</summary>

- Generator source: [`/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt)
- [`CodeGenSuppress` workaround documentation](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/docs/GUIDELINES.md)
- Related TypeSpec decorator: `@action` from `@azure-tools/typespec-azure-resource-manager`

</details>
